bug_1.title: Hardcoded biome — 7 of 8 biomes permanently inaccessible
bug_1.description: Every run unconditionally starts the coast biome. The BIOME_LABELS table at line 535 lists 8 biomes, the CameraRail and scene system both reference only CameraRail.createCoastalPath() and buildCoastScene(). No biome selection logic exists anywhere in the game loop. Players can never reach foret_broceliande, marais_korrigans, landes_bruyere, cercles_pierres, villages_celtes, collines_dolmens, or iles_mystiques.
bug_1.player_impact: 100% of runs are identical biome. Ogham/card variety associated with biome theming is never exercised.
bug_1.recommendation: Add a biome picker in the Lair (the 'map' zone already has a toast stub). Store the chosen biomeId in run state. Pass it to startRun(), buildScene(), CameraRail factory, and the toast. As interim fix even a random selection from the 8 keys at line 283-318 would unlock the full card pool.

bug_2.title: mg_herboristerie: aria-label built with empty targetPlant.name at canvas-creation time
bug_2.code_exact: this.canvas.setAttribute('aria-label', `Herboristerie — cliquez sur les plantes ${this.targetPlant.name || ''} et évitez les plantes toxiques`);
bug_2.description: this.targetPlant is initialised to { emoji: '', name: '' } at field declaration. buildGrid() (which sets the real targetPlant) is called at line 129, ten lines AFTER the canvas is created at line 117 and the aria-label is stamped at line 119. The label will always read 'Herboristerie — cliquez sur les plantes  et évitez les plantes toxiques' (empty name). The visual instruction label at line 89 correctly uses this.targetPlant after buildGrid, so the canvas itself plays correctly — only the accessibility label is wrong.
bug_2.player_impact: Screen-reader users receive no information about which plant to find. Low severity for sighted players, HIGH for accessibility.
bug_2.recommendation: Move the aria-label setAttribute call to after buildGrid() at line 129, or update it inside buildGrid() once this.targetPlant is assigned.

scene_1_main_ts.note: Same hardcoding appears at lines 283 (buildCoastScene call), 287 (CameraRail.createCoastalPath), and 318 (showBiomeToast). All four must be changed together.

validate_score_usage.detail: All 14 minigames call this.finish(score). MinigameBase.finish() always calls validateScore(score) at line 221 before any outcome computation, which clamps to [0, 100] and maps NaN/Infinity to 0. No minigame can deliver an out-of-range score to the game loop.

timer_timeout.detail: 11 of 14 minigames (all timed ones) use window.setInterval ticking timeLeft, calling this.endGame() when timeLeft <= 0. Round-based games (Course 8 rounds, Echo 6 rounds, Regard 3 rounds) end via prepareRound() guard when currentRound >= total. No infinite-loop risk found.

premature_finish_risk.detail: No minigame calls finish() or endGame() before the first user interaction opportunity. Timed games start their interval after setup() returns. Round-based games wait for rAF-driven phase transitions. The 'show' phase in Regard (3s memorisation) and the 'flash' phase in Echo (0.5s) correctly block click handling before transitioning to 'answer'.

score_range_analysis.mg_apaisement: 0 (no taps) or max(10, avgAccuracy * tapBonus * 100) — tapBonus caps at 1.0 so max is ~100. validateScore clamps. SAFE.
score_range_analysis.mg_combat_rituel: Math.min(100, survivalPct + centerPct). Explicit cap. SAFE.
score_range_analysis.mg_equilibre: zoneRatio * 100 where zoneRatio = timeInZone/totalElapsed. Max 100. SAFE.
score_range_analysis.mg_herboristerie: baseScore(80) + timeBonus(20) - penalty. Max 100 exactly. wrongPicks penalty can't go below 0 (Math.max(0,...)). SAFE.
score_range_analysis.mg_negociation: Math.min(100, Math.max(0, factionScore + comboBonus - penalty + timeBonus)). Explicit clamp. SAFE.
score_range_analysis.mg_ombres: maxProgress(0-1) * 100 * (1 - collisionPenalty*0.5). Max 100. validateScore also clamps. SAFE.

game_feel_distinct_mechanics.rationale: 14 minigames cover genuinely different input paradigms: rhythm tap (Apaisement), cursor dodge (Combat Rituel, Sang-Froid, Volonte, Ombres), QTE click (Course, Echo, Fouille), grid identification (Herboristerie), sequence memory (Regard), balance physics (Equilibre), word selection (Negociation). 5 of 14 share 'hold/move cursor' as core mechanic which creates some overlap feel between Combat Rituel, Sang-Froid, Volonte, and Ombres. Penalty -2.

player_feedback_obvious_before_overlay.rationale: All minigames show live score/status text (survivalTime, synchronisation%, focus%). Hit/miss feedback (colour flash, canvas dim, 'Trouve!') is immediate. The outcome overlay with 4 tiers (DESASTRE/ECHEC/REUSSITE/MAITRISE) adds a 2.2s narrative beat before resolving. Only gap: Regard and Echo give no instant visual cue on wrong click — the missed round just continues to the next.

balance_zero_without_input.rationale: Apaisement: 0 taps = finish(0). Correct. Course/Echo/Regard: 0 clicks = 0/total * 100 = finish(0). Correct. Combat Rituel: if player never moves cursor from starting center position, obstacles still rotate and will eventually hit — survivalTime will be short but not 0 (player starts at center). Fouille: no click = finish(0) when timer expires. Ombres: player starts at cursorX=30, which may or may not be inside the corridor at t=0; with no movement the score depends on corridor generation. Minor balance concern: Sang-Froid and Volonte initialise cursor at center (inside zone), so a completely idle player who never touches the mouse still accumulates timeInside for the duration before the zone drifts away — they could score 20-40 without any input on a slow drift. Penalty -3.

variety_14_minigames_feel_different.rationale: Structural variety is good (timer vs round-based, click vs move vs hold vs key). Visual variety is limited: all use the same dark canvas with Celtic colour palette (intentional thematic choice). The 'cursor follow' cluster (Combat Rituel, Sang-Froid, Volonte, Ombres) feels similar in practice despite different scoring logic. Penalty -3.

issues_summary.issue: Hardcoded biome 'cotes_sauvages' in main.ts at lines 283, 287, 316, 318 — 7/8 biomes inaccessible every run
issues_summary.recommendation: Implement biome selection in Lair 'map' zone or random pick from BIOME_LABELS keys at run start

issues_summary.issue: mg_herboristerie.ts line 119: canvas aria-label set before buildGrid() assigns targetPlant.name, always empty
issues_summary.recommendation: Move aria-label update to after buildGrid() call at line 129

issues_summary.issue: Sang-Froid and Volonte: idle player (no mouse movement) can score 20-40 for free due to cursor initialised inside zone and slow drift
issues_summary.recommendation: Require first pointer interaction to start timeInside accumulation, or start cursor outside safe zone

issues_summary.issue: Cursor-follow cluster (Combat Rituel, Sang-Froid, Volonte, Ombres): 4/14 minigames share the same input verb despite different scoring. Player perceives less variety than intended.
issues_summary.recommendation: Consider swapping one of the four for a keyboard-only mechanic. Equilibre already covers keyboard; Runes/Traces not verified — check those.

softlock_check.detail: All minigames have a timeout or round-completion path. MinigameBase.finish() has a double-call guard (this.finished = true). main.ts wraps minigame.play() in try/catch with neutral score=50 fallback. The game loop cannot be stuck by minigame failure.
